title: Bulbophyllum layardii
intro: Bulbophyllum layardii, also known as Layard's Bulbophyllum, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum layardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Sri Lankan medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the manufacture of herbal teas and infusions.
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum layardii is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Bulbophyllum is a large and diverse group, containing over 2,000 species. Bulbophyllum layardii is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands.
